treatments.name: CPAP therapy
treatments.effectiveness: Reduces apnea events by 80-90% in compliant patients
treatments.typical_timeline: Device setup within 1-2 weeks after diagnosis
treatments.summary: Continuous positive airway pressure is the gold-standard first-line treatment for moderate-to-severe OSA. Most insurers require prior auth and a DME supplier.

treatments.name: Oral appliance (mandibular advancement device)
treatments.effectiveness: Effective for mild-to-moderate OSA; 50-60% reduction in events
treatments.summary: Good option for patients who cannot tolerate CPAP. Requires dental specialist fitting.

treatments.name: UPPP surgery (uvulopalatopharyngoplasty)
treatments.effectiveness: 50-60% success rate; often combined with other procedures
treatments.typical_timeline: Surgery scheduling within 4-8 weeks after consult
treatments.summary: Surgical option for patients who fail CPAP. Higher cost, recovery time, and medical necessity review. Not first-line unless CPAP intolerable.

clinical_guidelines: AASM recommends CPAP as first-line for moderate-to-severe OSA
Surgery reserved for CPAP failure or specific anatomical indications
Prior authorization typically required for sleep studies and DME
